tools.name: apiosk_help
tools.description: Explain what Apiosk MCP is, how to connect it, how auth and USDC/x402 payments work, and the recommended workflow for discovery, wallets, and publishing.

tools.name: apiosk_discover
tools.description: Find the best paid x402 API for a data capability across discovery sources (Apiosk catalog + federated external listings). Decompose the user's request into capability segments first, then call this once per capability. Returns a normalized, ranked list; each result's `executable_via` says whether t

tools.name: apiosk_explore
tools.description: Browse Apiosk listing groups and explore one group at a time before narrowing with search.

tools.name: apiosk_inspect_x402
tools.description: Read an arbitrary URL's x402 payment terms (price, asset, network, payTo) WITHOUT paying. Use this on an external result's `url` from apiosk_discover before paying: it makes one unauthenticated request, parses the 402 offer, and returns the exact amount so you can confirm the price with the user. Re

tools.name: apiosk_execute
tools.description: Execute any Apiosk API by slug through the uniform /execute contract.

tools.name: apiosk_fetch_paid
tools.description: Pay an EXTERNAL x402 endpoint (one Apiosk does not host) from the connected wallet and return its data. Use this only for external results from apiosk_discover (executable_via='apiosk_fetch_paid'); for Apiosk catalog listings use apiosk_execute. REQUIRED: call apiosk_inspect_x402 on the url first, t

tools.name: apiosk_get_api
tools.description: Fetch full listing detail and agent metadata for a specific Apiosk API slug.

tools.name: apiosk_search
tools.description: Search and browse the Apiosk catalog by capability, price, or category. For browsing/filtering the catalog. When the goal is to fulfil a user request with real paid data ('get me the live X'), prefer apiosk_discover, which decomposes the need and ranks the best endpoints across sources.

tools.name: apiosk_payment_guide
tools.description: Explain how to pay through the Apiosk gateway. Returns a buyer guide (how an agent settles a paid API call over USDC/x402, tailored to the current auth) and a provider guide (how to publish an API and get paid). Pass slug to scope buyer guidance to one listing, or role to pick a side.

tools.name: apiosk_list_wallets
tools.description: List the signed-in user's managed Apiosk wallets. Requires an Apiosk dashboard session from local env auth or hosted MCP authorization.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "apiosk_list_wallets" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: List the signed-in user's managed Apiosk wallets. Requires an Apiosk dashboard session from local env auth or hosted MCP authorization.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: apiosk_inspect_x402, apiosk_fetch_paid · Private data: apiosk_fetch_paid · Public sink: apiosk_payment_guide · Destructive: apiosk_execute.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: apiosk_inspect_x402, apiosk_fetch_paid · Private data: apiosk_fetch_paid · Public sink: apiosk_payment_guide · Destructive: apiosk_execute

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
